title: Markdown, HTML and Javadoc tags should be consistent
description: Ensures that Markdown, HTML, and Javadoc tags are used consistently in Java code documentation. Inconsistent use of these tags can lead to confusion and reduce the clarity of documentation. This rule helps maintain a uniform style and improves the readability and maintainability of code documentation.
recommendation: Review all code documentation and ensure that Markdown, HTML, and Javadoc tags are used in a consistent and appropriate manner. Avoid mixing different tag styles within the same documentation block.
impact: Inconsistent documentation tags can confuse developers and reduce the effectiveness of code comments.

noncompliant: /** * This is a bold text. * This is a **bold** text. */
compliant: /** * This is a bold text. * This is a another bold text. */

checks.message: Review all code documentation and ensure that Markdown, HTML, and Javadoc tags are used in a consistent and appropriate manner. Avoid mixing different tag styles within the same documentation block.
